label: SOC Manager
description: Manages security operations center teams and programs — shift management, analyst development, SLA management, incident escalation, detection tuning strategy, and SOC program governance.

paths.name: SOC Management Track
paths.rationale: GSOM is the only dedicated SOC management certification. Combined with CISM and GCIL this is the most complete SOC leadership credential stack.
pros: GSOM is the only dedicated SOC manager certification — recognized by name in SOC leadership postings
CISM provides the security management framework expected at senior SOC leadership
GCIL adds incident leadership depth for SOC managers who lead during major breaches
Strong combination for enterprise SOC manager and MSSP SOC director roles
cons: All require significant security management experience
GSOM relatively new — building market recognition

prerequisites.experience: 5+ years security operations experience including 2+ years in a team lead or senior analyst role.

cert_sequence.notes: GIAC Cyber Incident Leadership — IR team management, stakeholder communication during incidents, and post-incident governance. Operational leadership foundation for SOC managers who escalate and lead during major incidents.

GIAC Security Operations Manager — the only dedicated SOC management certification. SOC architecture design, metrics and KPIs, analyst development, playbook governance, and SOC program management. Associated with SANS LDR551. Most directly relevant credential for the SOC manager title.

ISACA Certified Information Security Manager — adds security management framework recognition expected at senior SOC leadership. CISM alongside GSOM is the recognized senior SOC leadership credential combination.
